fields.description: The bare DNS label Central chose, e.g. acme. A single label (no dots) so the pilot stays inside the one regional wildcard the proxy already terminates. The pilot is fronted at <subdomain>.<region domain> — that FQDN is this row's name and the proxy Host header. Same Contract-A rule a self-serve Site uses.
fields.label: Subdomain

fields.description: The Central team this pilot belongs to. Stamped by the Central-facing create_vm API; set once at insert. Operator/e2e pilots created directly may leave it empty.
fields.label: Tenant

fields.description: Controller-written lifecycle state. Running is reached only after the backing VM boots and the in-guest deploy mints the one-click login URL. Pending → Running; Failed on any error; Terminated after teardown.
fields.label: Status

fields.description: Which bench front door the baked image serves: admin (the bench admin console, minted via `bench generate-admin-session`) or site (a single baked site, minted via `bench browse`). Inherited from the backing VM's image at insert; drives the login-URL mint mode and its TTL.
fields.label: Build Mode

fields.description: The Virtual Machine this pilot boots and deploys into, cloned from a bench image by the controller (Central never picks it). Immutable once chosen. Plain VM facts (ipv6, sizing) are read through this link — the pilot stores only its bench-level state.
fields.label: Virtual Machine

fields.description: The Subdomain (proxy map) row this pilot created once its backing VM booted and deployed — deleting it takes the pilot off the front door. Written by the controller after the guest deploy mints the login URL. Same routing entry a self-serve Site creates.
fields.label: Routing Entry

fields.description: The one-click admin sign-in URL minted after boot (bench generate-admin-session / bench browse). Short-lived and single-use — see login_url_expires_at; Central regenerates it if a tenant clicks after it expires. Surfaced to Central (event payload + poll) once Running. Controller-written.
fields.label: Login URL

fields.description: When login_url stops working (mint time + the mint mode's TTL: 5 min for admin's single-use JWT, 24h for a site session). Central compares against this to decide 'use it' vs 'regenerate'. Stamped alongside login_url; empty until Running.
fields.label: Login URL Expires At
